todos.detail: IP name: arm_cpu, revision, author, date. Summary: ARMv4-compatible 32-bit CPU, 3-stage pipeline (FETCH/DECODE-EXECUTE/MEMORY-WRITEBACK), SystemVerilog, ~1400 RTL lines, 11 modules + 1 package. Target: FPGA synthesis. Source: mas/arm_cpu_mas.md

Extract all ports from arm_cpu_top.sv: clk, rst_n (active-low), gpio_out[31:0], gpio_in[31:0], debug_pc[31:0], debug_instr[31:0], debug_state[3:0], debug_reg_r0-r3[31:0], running. Document direction, width, reset value, description. Note: debug_reg_r0-r3 are currently stubbed to 0 in RTL — flag as known issue.

ASCII block diagram showing: arm_cpu_top -> arm_cpu_core + arm_imem + arm_dmem. Core internals: PC -> IMEM fetch -> Decoder -> Condition Check -> RegFile (dual read) -> Barrel Shifter -> ALU -> Write-back mux -> RegFile write. CPSR feeds back to condition check and shifter carry. Control FSM drives all enable/mux signals. Describe 3-stage pipeline: FETCH (PC+imem), DECODE-EXECUTE (decode+shifter+ALU), MEMORY-WRITEBACK (dmem access + reg write). 5 FSM states: FETCH/DECODE/EXECUTE/MEMORY/WRITEBACK. Load/store inserts MEMORY state; all others skip it.

todos.content: Write MAS §4 — Module Inventory (11 modules + 1 package)
todos.detail: For each module list: file path, port table (name/dir/width/desc), key parameters, line count. Modules: arm_defs (package, rtl/arm_decoder.sv lines 12-42), arm_reg_file (rtl/arm_reg_file.sv, 52 lines, 16x32 reg, SP init=0x1000), arm_alu (rtl/arm_alu.sv, 174 lines, 16 ops, NZCV), arm_barrel_shifter (rtl/arm_barrel_shifter.sv, 125 lines, LSL/LSR/ASR/ROR/RRX), arm_decoder (rtl/arm_decoder.sv, 166 lines, 18 output control signals), arm_condition_check (rtl/arm_condition_check.sv, 39 lines, 15 condition codes), arm_cpsr (rtl/arm_cpsr.sv, 87 lines, NZCV+I/F/T/M), arm_control (rtl/arm_control.sv, 184 lines, 5-state FSM, 15 output control signals), arm_imem (rtl/arm_imem.sv, 26 lines, 4KB $readmemh), arm_dmem (rtl/arm_dmem.sv, 73 lines, 4KB byte-enable), arm_cpu_core (rtl/arm_cpu_core.sv, 384 lines, datapath integration), arm_cpu_top (rtl/arm_cpu_top.sv, 119 lines, memories + GPIO). Total hierarchy tree.

todos.content: Write MAS §5 — Instruction Set Support Matrix
todos.detail: Table per instruction class with: mnemonic, opcode[24:21], encoding bits[27:25], RTL status (implemented/partial/stub). Data Processing: AND(0h)/EOR(1h)/SUB(2h)/RSB(3h)/ADD(4h)/ADC(5h)/SBC(6h)/RSC(7h)/TST(8h)/TEQ(9h)/CMP(Ah)/CMN(Bh)/ORR(Ch)/MOV(Dh)/BIC(Eh)/MVN(Fh) — all implemented. Load/Store: LDR/STR/LDRB/STRB — implemented (imm offset only, register offset decoded but no shifter path). Branch: B/BL — implemented (link register write has bug, see §8). Block Transfer: LDM/STM — decoded only, no execute logic. MUL: detected by decoder but no execute in control. MRS/MSR: decoded, MRS uses result_sel=2'b00 (ALU path), MSR writes via msr_we. SWI: decoded, control sets pc_sel=1 but no vector table. Condition codes: all 15 (EQ/NE/CS/CC/MI/PL/VS/VC/HI/LS/GE/LT/GT/LE/AL). Barrel shifter modes: LSL/LSR/ASR/ROR/RRX — all implemented. Note: immediate rotate uses shift_amount[3:0] not 2*rotate_imm.

Describe each datapath path: (1) Data Processing: Rn->rf_rdata_a, Rm->rf_rdata_b->shifter->alu_op_b, ALU->wb_result->reg write. (2) Immediate: operand2[7:0]->shifter (ROR by operand2[11:8])->ALU. (3) Branch: signed_offset[23:0] sign-extend<<2 + PC+8 -> branch_target -> PC. (4) Load: Rn+sign-extend(ls_offset) -> dmem_addr, dmem_rdata -> wb_result(result_sel=01) -> reg write. (5) Store: Rn+offset -> dmem_addr, rf_rdata_b(Rm) -> dmem_wdata. (6) MRS: cpsr_out -> wb_result(result_sel=10) -> reg write. (7) MSR: rf_rdata_a -> msr_data -> CPSR. Key muxes: rf_raddr_b = operand2[3:0] (Rm), shifter_operand = imm ? {24'd0,operand2[7:0]} : rf_rdata_b, wb_result = result_sel ? (01=mem, 10=cpsr) : alu_result. PC read as R15 = pc_out from reg_file.

todos.content: Write MAS §7 — Control FSM & Signal Table
todos.detail: FSM states: FETCH(000)/DECODE(001)/EXECUTE(010)/MEMORY(011)/WRITEBACK(100). State transitions: FETCH->DECODE always. DECODE->EXECUTE always. EXECUTE->MEMORY if is_load_store && cond_pass, else ->WRITEBACK. MEMORY->WRITEBACK always, stall=1. WRITEBACK->FETCH always. Control signal table per state: FETCH (all zero), DECODE (shifter_en=is_data_proc|is_load_store|is_mul, shift_imm_sel=is_imm_op2), EXECUTE (alu_op_en=1, branch: pc_sel/pc_we/lr_we, data_proc: regfile_we+result_sel=00, msr/mrs), MEMORY (mem_req=1, mem_we=is_store, mem_byte=is_byte), WRITEBACK (load: regfile_we+result_sel=01, PC: pc_we=1 if not taken branch). List all 15 control outputs with default values.

todos.content: Write MAS §8 — RTL Notes, Issues & Warnings
todos.detail: Known RTL issues extracted from code review: (1) BUG: lr_we block in arm_cpu_core.sv lines 339-345 is empty — BL instruction never writes PC+4 to R14. (2) BUG: debug_reg_r0-r3 tied to 32'd0 — not connected to register file debug ports. (3) UNUSED: pc_next declared but never assigned/used in arm_cpu_core. (4) UNUSED: mul_result[63:0] declared but never used in arm_alu. (5) UNCONNECTED: pc_out of arm_reg_file left floating (unconnected) in arm_cpu_core instantiation. (6) DEVIATION: Barrel shifter is_imm path uses shift_amount[3:0] instead of {shift_amount[4:0],1'b0} (2*rotate_imm per ARM spec). (7) MISMAP: Load/store decoder maps U-bit (bit 23) to is_pre_index name — confusing; U-bit is Up/Down offset direction, P-bit (bit 24) is Pre/Post index. (8) MISSING: LDM/STM decoded but no execute/writeback logic in arm_control. (9) MISSING: MUL detected but no execute logic. (10) MISSING: SWI sets pc_sel=1 but branch_target is not the SWI vector (0x08). (11) POST-INDEX: Load/store post-index path uses ls_base_addr directly but never applies offset after access. (12) WARNINGS: 18 pyslang warnings (signedness, missing defaults, bit-width expansion) — all non-functional but should be cleaned. (13) Store data path uses rf_rdata_b = registers[operand2[3:0]] which is Rm — correct for store but operand2[3:0] is shared with shift operand, potential mux conflict for register-offset loads.

Existing TB: tb/arm_cpu_tb.sv (308 lines). Test program: arm_program.hex generated at runtime via $fwrite. Coverage: MOV imm, ADD, SUB, AND, ORR, CMP+BEQ, BL+return (MOV PC,LR), STR/LDR, EOR, MVN, BIC, B infinite loop. 35 instructions total. Gaps: (1) No register visibility — debug_reg_r0-r3 tied to 0, cannot verify results. (2) No flag checks — NZCV never inspected after CMP/ALU. (3) No halfword tests. (4) No writeback-address tests (STR with writeback). (5) No negative/deprecated instruction tests. (6) No boundary tests (shift by 0, shift by 32). (7) No multiple-condition tests (NE, GT, LT etc). (8) No memory-mapped IO GPIO tests. Recommended fixes: (a) Add debug register read port to arm_reg_file (4 extra outputs or scan interface). (b) Add timed checks after each instruction group with expected register values. (c) Add flag check task. (d) Add test cases for: ADC/SBC/RSC with known carry, LDRB/STRB, shift-boundary (LSL#0, LSR#32, ASR#32, ROR#16), conditional branches (NE, GT, LT, GE, LE), multiple BL nesting. (e) Add pass/fail summary with specific register value checks.

todos.content: Write MAS §10 — Hierarchy Tree & File List
todos.detail: Full hierarchy: arm_cpu_top -> arm_cpu_core -> {arm_decoder, arm_condition_check, arm_cpsr, arm_control, arm_reg_file, arm_barrel_shifter, arm_alu} + arm_imem + arm_dmem. File list with paths and compile order: arm_defs package (inside arm_decoder.sv) must be compiled first. Compile order: arm_decoder.sv (has package), arm_reg_file.sv, arm_barrel_shifter.sv, arm_alu.sv, arm_condition_check.sv, arm_cpsr.sv, arm_control.sv, arm_imem.sv, arm_dmem.sv, arm_cpu_core.sv, arm_cpu_top.sv.
